title: myosotis cespitosa subsp. repens
intro: Myosotis cespitosa subsp. repens, also known as creeping forget-me-not, is a low-growing, creeping perennial plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Myosotis cespitosa subsp. repens include its potential use in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
